title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · Operations Defense
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a distribution-aware package change record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.

content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Package Rollback symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GNU Bash Reference Manual — Controls Bash parsing, expansion, quoting, redirection, pipelines, startup, job control, scripting, and error behavior.

hints: Name the Package Rollback precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Host proposes lab-tool 5.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 5.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Uninstalling a package always restores the machine to its previous state.

stimulus.title: Package Rollback failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Uninstalling a package always restores the machine to its previous state.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Package Manager Model evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Package Manager Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Host proposes lab-tool 6.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 6.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Package Manager Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Package Manager Model precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Package Rollback: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Host proposes lab-tool 2.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 2.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Plan configuration preservation, cache use, downgrade constraints, service impact, and verification for package rollback.
